resposta:
solution:

```python
class Solution:
    def findAllPeople(self, n: int, meetings: List[List[int]], firstPerson: int) -> List[int]:
        seen = set()
        result = []
        for meeting in meetings:
            x, y, time = meeting
            if x not in seen and y not in seen:
                seen.add(x)
                seen.add(y)
                result.append(x)
                result.append(y)
        return result
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW

Explanation:

The problem involves finding all the people who know the secret after the meetings have taken place. We can solve this problem using a set to keep track of the people we have seen so far. We iterate through the meetings and for each meeting, check if the meeting participants (x and y) are not in the seen set. If they are not, add them to the set and add them to the result list. The time complexity is O(n) because we iterate through n meetings. The space complexity is O(n) because we use a set to store the seen people. The energy implications are low because the algorithm only requires a small amount of energy to execute.
